summary: Implemented Timer Panel UI components for workout execution: TimerPanel (slide-up rest timer with countdown display, circular progress indicator, -30s/skip/+30s adjustment buttons, start-next-set button, expired recovery message), CircularProgress (ring progress indicator), and timer-helpers (pure functions for phase computation, progress calculation, notification text formatting, and visibility logic).
keyDecisions: Extracted timer panel logic into pure helper functions (timer-helpers.ts) for testability, following the pattern established by workout-helpers.ts
Used TimerPanelPhase discriminated union type ('hidden'|'counting'|'completed'|'expired') to model all timer visual states
CircularProgress uses View-based half-circle rotation transforms instead of SVG for React Native compatibility
TimerPanel receives phase as a prop (computed by parent via getTimerPanelPhase) rather than computing it internally, enabling clean separation of state computation from rendering
Timer text uses 72px SF Pro Display Bold (Typography.timerDisplay token) with tabular-nums for stable width during countdown

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Timer panel slides up from bottom after set completion with large countdown display (72px)
Countdown reaches 0 - text turns red + vibration + sound alert (text red implemented, vibration/sound delegated to parent)
"开始下一组" button appears on countdown completion
Timer counts in foreground while other exercise cards dim (panel renders over cards, dimming handled by parent)
System notification shows remaining time when app backgrounded (formatNotificationText helper implemented)
Tapping notification returns to workout page with "开始下一组" ready (navigation handled by parent)
Lock screen shows notification on timer completion (integration via existing timer service)
Phone call / long absence: shows expired recovery message on return
Force-close recovery: app reopens to workout page with timer state computed from timestamp (uses existing timer service recoverState)
Timer auto-cancels on workout exit (handled by workout store timerService.skip())

notes: TimerPanel is a presentational component that integrates with the existing useTimer hook and timerStore. Background notification, vibration, and sound alerts are handled at the integration/service layer (existing timer.ts and timer.store.ts). The component provides the visual layer with phase-based rendering.
